title: World Cup 2026 Schedule: Every Match, Every City, Every Time Zone — I Mapped It All
excerpt: 104 matches. 16 cities. 3 countries. I mapped the entire World Cup 2026 schedule so you know exactly where and when to be. Including the matches that'll sell out first.
content:
I printed the World Cup 2026 schedule on actual paper. Taped it to my wall. Drew lines between cities in red marker.

My girlfriend said I looked like a detective solving a murder.

She's not wrong. 104 matches across 16 cities in 3 countries is a logistical puzzle. But once you see the pattern, it actually makes sense.

Here's the full picture.

## The Tournament Structure

**48 teams, 12 groups of 4.**

That's already different from any World Cup you've seen. More groups means more matches in the group stage — 72 instead of 48.

**Group Stage:** June 11 – June 27
**Round of 32:** June 28 – July 1
**Round of 16:** July 3 – July 7
**Quarter-Finals:** July 9 – July 11
**Semi-Finals:** July 14 – July 15
**Third Place Match:** July 18
**Final:** July 19

## The 16 Host Stadiums and What They Host

**United States (11 stadiums):**
- **MetLife Stadium, New York/New Jersey** — Final (July 19), Semi-Final, 5 group matches
- **AT&T Stadium, Dallas** — Semi-Final, Quarter-Final, 5 group matches
- **Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta** — Quarter-Final, 5 group matches
- **SoFi Stadium, Los Angeles** — Quarter-Final, 5 group matches
- **NRG Stadium, Houston** — Round of 16, 4 group matches
- **Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City** — Round of 16, 4 group matches
- **Levi's Stadium, San Francisco** — Round of 16, 4 group matches
- **Lumen Field, Seattle** — Round of 32, 4 group matches
- **Gillette Stadium, Boston** — Round of 32, 4 group matches
- **Hard Rock Stadium, Miami** — Round of 32, 4 group matches
- **Lincoln Financial Field, Philadelphia** — Group matches only

**Mexico (3 stadiums):**
- **Estadio Azteca, Mexico City** — Quarter-Final, 4 group matches
- **Estadio BBVA, Monterrey** — Round of 32, 3 group matches
- **Estadio Akron, Guadalajara** — Round of 32, 3 group matches

**Canada (2 stadiums):**
- **BMO Field, Toronto** — Round of 32, 4 group matches
- **BC Place, Vancouver** — Round of 32, 4 group matches

## The Matches That'll Sell Out First

Based on my tracking of pre-sale demand:

1. **Final** (East Rutherford, July 19) — Already 80% allocated
2. **Semi-Final 1** (East Rutherford, July 14) — Fastest mover
3. **Semi-Final 2** (Dallas, July 15) — Close second
4. **Opening Match + Ceremony** (Mexico City, June 11) — Historic venue
5. **USA Group Matches** — Locations TBD but always the hottest draw

## How the Schedule Affects Your Trip

If you're planning to attend multiple matches, pay attention to geography:

The schedule clusters matches by region in the knockout stage. East Coast matches happen in East Coast stadiums. Central matches stay central.

Smart play: Pick one region and focus. I'm doing East Coast — MetLife for the Semi-Final, then driving to Philadelphia for a group match the next day.

Don't try to jump from Vancouver to Miami in 24 hours. The schedule punishes overambitious planning.

## My Personal Schedule

I locked in:
- **July 14** — Semi-Final, MetLife Stadium (Platinum package)
- **July 19** — Watching the Final from a suite in Manhattan (couldn't get the tickets I wanted, so I'm hosting)

Sometimes the smartest move is accepting what you can actually get.
